title: Notification Queue
description:
Build a **notification queue** system using `useReducer` and `useEffect` that supports adding notifications, auto-dismiss, and manual dismiss.

### Requirements

- `notificationReducer` handles: `ADD`, `DISMISS`, `DISMISS_ALL`
- Each notification has: `id`, `message`, `type` (`'info'` | `'success'` | `'error'`), `duration` (ms, default 3000)
- `ADD`: Append notification to the queue
- `DISMISS`: Remove notification by id
- `DISMISS_ALL`: Clear all notifications
- Notifications auto-dismiss after their `duration` expires
- Maximum 5 visible notifications (new ones replace oldest if limit exceeded)
- Implement a `useNotifications()` hook that returns `{ notifications, addNotification, dismiss, dismissAll }`

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Notification item | `notification-{id}` |
| Dismiss button | `dismiss-{id}` |
| Dismiss all button | `dismiss-all` |
| Add info button | `add-info` |
| Add success button | `add-success` |
| Add error button | `add-error` |
| Notification count | `count` |
constraints:
- Use useReducer for notification state
- Auto-dismiss must clean up timeouts on unmount and when manually dismissed
- Maximum of 5 notifications visible at once
- Each notification must have a unique id
hints: Use useEffect with the notifications array as dependency — set up timeouts for each notification that hasn't expired.
Store a Map or Set of timeout IDs in a ref so you can clear them when notifications are dismissed manually.
For the max limit, slice the array after adding: if length > 5, keep only the last 5.
